title: Executive Function - Fill in the Blank

questions.question: Executive function is built on two foundational concepts: rules and ___.
questions.blank_sentence: Executive function is built on two foundational concepts: rules and ___.
questions.explanation: Nearly every definition of executive function rests on rules (abstract, flexible guides for behaviour) and control (engaging the appropriate rule for the current context). The 'rules + control' pair underwrites the entire taxonomy.

questions.question: The oddball task generates a positive ERP deflection beginning around 300 ms post-target called the ___.
questions.blank_sentence: The oddball task generates a positive ERP deflection beginning around 300 ms post-target called the ___.
questions.explanation: The P300 is the positive ERP deflection generated by oddball stimuli ~300 ms post-target. It is unusually robust — picked up with relatively little data — and is the basis of the Module 5 practical.

questions.question: The classic case of frontal disinhibition syndrome was the railroad worker ___, whose tamping iron damaged his orbitofrontal and medial PFC.
questions.blank_sentence: The classic case of frontal disinhibition syndrome was the railroad worker ___, whose tamping iron damaged his orbitofrontal and medial PFC.
questions.explanation: Phineas Gage is the classic case of disinhibition syndrome — the tamping iron passed through his orbitofrontal and medial PFC. The lecture noted the 'reckless and profane' narrative is now thought to be exaggerated.

questions.question: First reported by Brenda Milner in 1963, the inability of PFC patients to shift away from a previously valid sorting rule is called ___.
questions.blank_sentence: First reported by Brenda Milner in 1963, the inability of PFC patients to shift away from a previously valid sorting rule is called ___.
questions.explanation: Perseveration is the hallmark of PFC dysfunction on the Wisconsin Card Sorting Test — continuing to use the previously valid rule despite negative feedback. Brenda Milner first documented this in 1963.

questions.question: The mediodorsal nucleus of the ___ is the relay station that loops basal-ganglia output back to the cortex.
questions.blank_sentence: The mediodorsal nucleus of the ___ is the relay station that loops basal-ganglia output back to the cortex.
questions.explanation: The mediodorsal thalamic nucleus closes the cortico-striatal-thalamo-cortical loop. The professor stressed the thalamus is often left out of figures but is central to making the circuit a closed loop, not a one-way street.

questions.question: The anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) is most associated with ___ monitoring — detecting competing responses and signalling the need for control.
questions.blank_sentence: The anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) is most associated with ___ monitoring — detecting competing responses and signalling the need for control.
questions.explanation: The ACC's classical role is conflict monitoring — detecting competing responses (e.g., on incongruent Stroop trials), processing error feedback, and allocating control resources to the DLPFC.

questions.question: The ___ cortex is critical for shifting in tasks that involve reward and punishment, such as reversal learning.
questions.blank_sentence: The ___ cortex is critical for shifting in tasks that involve reward and punishment, such as reversal learning.
questions.explanation: The orbitofrontal cortex (OFC) is critical for reversal learning. OFC-lesioned animals learn stimulus-reward contingencies normally but cannot relearn when contingencies are reversed.

questions.question: In the rostro-caudal hierarchy theories, ___'s temporal abstraction model proposes that posterior PFC handles current-time rules while anterior PFC handles long-term goal rules.
questions.blank_sentence: In the rostro-caudal hierarchy theories, ___'s temporal abstraction model proposes that posterior PFC handles current-time rules while anterior PFC handles long-term goal rules.
questions.explanation: Etienne Koechlin's temporal abstraction theory organises the PFC by time horizon: posterior = current-time rules, middle = changing-context rules, anterior = long-term goal rules. This contrasts with Badre & D'Esposito's policy abstraction account.

questions.question: The cutting-edge 7T fMRI meditation paper showed reduced activity in the ___ Network during focused meditation versus passive rest.
questions.blank_sentence: The cutting-edge 7T fMRI meditation paper showed reduced activity in the ___ Network during focused meditation versus passive rest.
questions.explanation: The 7T study found reduced activity in the Default Mode Network (also called the task-negative network) during focused meditation compared to passive rest. Core DMN nodes are the posterior cingulate cortex and medial PFC.

questions.question: The frontopolar cortex sits at the most ___ tip of the prefrontal cortex and is implicated in relating rules and tracking higher-order goals.
questions.blank_sentence: The frontopolar cortex sits at the most ___ tip of the prefrontal cortex and is implicated in relating rules and tracking higher-order goals.
questions.explanation: The frontopolar cortex is the most anterior (rostral) tip of the PFC. It activates specifically for relating rules, higher-order integration, tracking goals/subgoals, and exploratory information-seeking behaviour.
